text.title: Microservice Structure and FastAPI Skeleton
text.summary: Per-service folder layout and FastAPI service entrypoint with lifespan, health check, and versioned API router.

rule.statement: Each service is a completely independent deployment unit: its own repository (or isolated module), its own Dockerfile, its own database, its own CI/CD pipeline.
rule.rationale: Shared infrastructure creates hidden coupling. A database schema change in a shared DB can break all services that read it, defeating independent deployability.

rule.statement: Every service exposes a /health endpoint. The health check returns 200 only when the service and its critical dependencies (DB, broker) are reachable.
rule.rationale: Load balancers and orchestrators use health checks to route traffic. A service that responds 200 while its DB is down accepts requests it cannot fulfill.

example.code: user-service/ ├── src/ │ ├── api/ │ │ ├── routes.py # FastAPI routers │ │ └── schemas.py # Request/response Pydantic models │ ├── domain/ │ │ ├── entities.py # Domain entities │ │ ├── services.py # Domain services │ │ └── events.py # Integration events │ ├── infrastructure/ │ │ ├── database.py # DB session factory │ │ └── message_bus.py # Broker producer/consumer │ └── main.py # FastAPI app + lifespan ├── tests/ ├── Dockerfile └── requirements.txt

example.note: Lifespan replaces @app.on_event deprecated hooks. Startup and shutdown logic are co-located and symmetric.

text.reference: Complete FastAPI service entrypoint with health check
